evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Multiple likely-fabricated package names and import paths: '@better-auth/prisma-adapter', '@better-auth/plugin-magic-link', '@better-auth/infra', '@better-auth/test-utils', 'better-auth/providers/google', 'better-auth/next'. Real better-auth uses 'better-auth/adapters/prisma', 'better-auth/plugins' for magicLink, social providers configured inline (no separate import), and 'better-auth/next-js' for toNextJsHandler/nextCookies. Playbook hedges with 'confirm exact package names' which signals uncertainty. No version pin stated.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse syntactically but imports will fail at install/runtime due to non-existent packages. Multiple code samples; extractor would write 'src/lib/auth.ts' etc. but pip/npm installs reference packages that don't exist. No single coherent test command. Multiple alternative install snippets (npm vs pnpm) create extractor ambiguity.
evidence.completeness: Has (a) Google credential acquisition mentioned, (b) install commands in bash blocks (but contradictory npm/pnpm), (c) env template present, (d) backend code present, (e) frontend code present, (f) no clear runnable test command — only narrative about Playwright/Vitest. Heavy duplication and conflicting samples. Env vars listed match those referenced in code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Uses bracketed numeric references [1][19] etc. with no URLs resolved. No canonical clerk.com-equivalent (better-auth.com/docs) URLs present anywhere. No official docs URLs at all.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true → long-tail handling applied. Internal inconsistency: playbook itself repeatedly tells reader to 'confirm package names', signaling the author is uncertain — penalized under consistency. Massive length with much duplicated narrative content; extractor would face multiple competing code samples.
